entries.title: Hawks Coordinates Against AFO While Damaged

context.label: Gunga battle against All For One begins

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Hawks remains tactically important against All For One despite severe limits after earlier injuries.
content.injection: Use Hawks as a strategist and wounded combatant; do not restore him to effortless pre-war peak.

entries.title: AFO Uses Quirk Theft As Psychological Terror

context.label: Hawks, Tokoyami, and Jiro face All For One

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: All For One’s stolen Quirks and history make his presence psychologically crushing as well as physically dangerous.
content.injection: Portray AFO as predatory and layered; his threat is theft, mockery, pressure, and accumulated power.

entries.title: Endeavor Damages AFO Through Resolve Not Redemption

context.label: Endeavor wounds All For One at Gunga

content.fact: Endeavor’s successful attack on All For One comes from desperate resolve, not from his family sins being forgiven.
content.injection: Do not treat Endeavor’s battlefield grit as completed redemption; keep hero duty separate from family repair.

entries.title: AFO Rewind Regression Is A Time Limit

context.label: All For One begins Rewind regression

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: All For One restores and rewinds his body with a Rewind-derived effect that also makes his remaining time finite.
content.injection: After the Rewind effect begins, AFO becomes physically younger and more dangerous but is racing his own disappearance.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

entries.title: Younger AFO Is More Reckless And Narcissistic

content.fact: As AFO regresses, his arrogance and fixation on reaching Shigaraki intensify.
content.injection: Write regressing AFO as powerful but increasingly tunnel-visioned; he burns time to reach his vessel.

content.injection: After this anchor, the clean separation plan should feel compromised; Kurogiri is both person and portal threat.

context.label: Spinner reaches Kurogiri at Central Hospital

content.fact: Spinner’s role at the hospital is tied to exploited heteromorph resentment as well as League loyalty.
content.injection: Avoid portraying the hospital riot as mindless monster violence only; grievance is real, even when weaponized.

entries.title: AFO Body Ending Is Not The Same As AFO Influence Ending

context.label: All For One begins Rewind regression

content.fact: All For One’s rewinding body can be defeated while his deeper influence through Shigaraki remains unresolved.
content.injection: Do not end the whole villain threat just because AFO’s original body is failing; the vessel conflict remains active.

entries.title: AFO Regression Makes Victory A Countdown

context.label: All For One begins Rewind regression
